records.ety: [F. dput, fr. LL. deputatus. See Depute.]

defs: One appointed as the substitute of another, and empowered to act for him, in his name or his behalf; a substitute in office; a lieutenant; a representative; a delegate; a vicegerent; as, the deputy of a prince, of a sheriff, of a township, etc.
quotes: There was then [in the days of Jehoshaphat] no king in Edom; a deputy was king.
God's substitute, His deputy anointed in His sight.
notes: ☞ Deputy is used in combination with the names of various executive officers, to denote an assistant empowered to act in their name; as, deputy collector, deputy marshal, deputy sheriff.

defs: A member of the Chamber of Deputies.
